title: Terminal line malformed after pressing HOME/END
body:
### Issue Description

I am running bash inside of an podman container (toolbox at the moment, but I'm experiencing it with `podman exec -it cnt bash` this issues as well. It looks like when I reach certain line length, then pressing HOME/END and sometimes also arrows moves the beginning and end of the line to different position. It is then impossible to edit the line.

If I have the following line and prese HOME and END then the cursor position if swong:
```
tb-authselect [pbrezina /dev/shm/authselect]$ ~/workspace/authselect/configure --enable-silent-rules --prefix=/dev/shm/authselect-inst
                                                         ^ HOME                                                                                  ^ END
```

Note that I did not put any spaces after the text. Only the cursor was moved there.

It makes it impossible to do any serious work within the container. I was experiencing this years before, I think there was already ticket opened for it, but couldn't find it. It was not a huge issue for me since I did not work inside containers a lots, but now I'm switching to toolbox workflow and it is a pain.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Open bash inside a container (`toolbox enter`, `podman exec -it cnt bash`)
2. Write a long enough line
3.  Press HOME and END


### Describe the results you received

HOME does not jump to the line start and END jumps behind the line end. It is not possible to edit the line correctly.

### Describe the results you expected

HOME and END works as expected.

comments.body:
Actually @debarshiray got me thinking that it may be a wrong prompt and indeed. I have colors in my prompt as this:

```
prefix=""

if [ -f /run/.containerenv ]; then
    prefix=`source /run/.containerenv && echo "\e[1;34m$name\e[0m "`
fi
export PS1="$prefix[\u \w$MY_GIT_PROMPT]$MY_PS1_SIGN "
```

It prints the container name in blue. Apparently, this is causing the issue and even on localhost. The color is probably wrongly formatted.

Closing this as not a podman bug.

EDIT: I was missing brackets around the color sequence `\[\e[...m\]`. It works now.
I am glad you managed to track it down!  :)
